steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The first sentence sets up a struggle: Dillard struggles to reconcile the juxtapositions of nature. The second sentence — after the blank — gives her final understanding: intricacy and harshness are inextricably linked. The transition must mark this move from ongoing struggle to a concluding insight.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "Ultimately,": signals the final outcome reached after working through something — perfect for a conclusion that follows from a described struggle. ✓
  • "To that end,": signals that what follows is a method or action taken to achieve the goal just mentioned — but Dillard’s realization is not a method she uses; it is where she arrives. ✗
  • "Moreover,": signals adding another independent point, implying the second sentence introduces new information rather than a resolution of the first. ✗
  • "Hence,": signals a logical consequence, as if the second sentence follows mechanically from the first — but the passage frames this as a hard-won narrative insight, not a direct logical deduction. ✗
That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
